notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 2, Book 12 source file.
Strong Reader card: longer text and more inferential fable work.

book.title: The Farmer and the Stork
book.description: The Farmer and the Stork includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: longer text and more inferential fable work

weeks.title: The Farmer and the Stork Book Card
reading: Chapter 6: The Price of Bad Company
vocabulary: 1. agriculturalists - people who work in farming and growing crops
1. systematically - in an organized and methodical way
1. drastic - extreme measures taken to solve a serious problem
2. retreated - moved back to a safe place
2. wrongdoers - people or animals who do bad or harmful things
2. associate - to spend time with or be connected to someone
1. blameless - free from fault or responsibility for wrongdoing
2. associations - connections or relationships with other people
journal: Write about why choosing friends carefully matters.
Explain whether the stork was treated fairly. Use evidence.
writing: Write about why choosing friends carefully matters.
Explain whether the stork was treated fairly. Use evidence.
questions: 2. What was the farmer most proud of?
1. What problem did the farmer discover?
2. How did the cranes behave in the farmer's fields?
1. What solution did the farmer decide to try?
2. How did the farmer prepare his trap?
1. What was the stork looking for in the field?
2. How was the stork different from the cranes?
1. How did the stork try to defend herself?
2. Why didn't the farmer believe the stork?
1. What lesson does the stork's situation teach us?
2. What does this story remind us about the world?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
